mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate above 6.5% signals deteriorating labor market conditions, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 6.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous increase in industrial production (INDPRO) and GDP (GDPC1) indicates robust economic growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns are not significantly positive when both INDPRO and GDPC1 rise by >1% sequentially.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of high inflation (CPIAUCSL > 220), central banks may tighten monetary policy, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 60% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 220.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in housing starts below 900 thousand units signals weak demand, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of instances where HOUST falls below 900.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflects a strong labor market, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns are not significantly positive when PAYEMS grows by >100k sequentially.

mechanism_narrative: A concurrent increase in GDP (GDPC1) and retail sales (RSAFS) signals strong consumer demand, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns are not significantly positive when both GDPC1 and RSAFS rise by >1% sequentially.

mechanism_narrative: During supply chain disruptions (INDPRO < 105), production declines, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 60% of instances where INDPRO falls below 105.
